Class DialogManager

java.lang.Object
util.DialogManager
public class DialogManager extends Object
Manages dialogs in the game.

The dialog system supports special formatting tags embedded in the text to trigger animations, sound effects, pauses, and text styling during rendering. Formatting tags are enclosed in $ characters.

Supported Tags:

  • $P=milliseconds$ - Pauses the text generation for the specified duration (e.g., $P=500$).
  • $S=filename$ - Plays a sound effect (e.g., $S=gasp.wav$).
  • $I=filename$ - Displays a tutorial image (e.g., $I=step1.png$).
  • $F=expression$ - Changes the character's face expression (e.g., $F=neutral$).
  • $C#hexcolor,text$ - Colors the enclosed text with the given hex color code (e.g., $C#FF0000,Warning!$).

      A flag indicating if a dialog animation is currently playing.

  • Constructor Details

    • DialogManager

      public DialogManager()
      Default constructor for DialogManager.

  • Method Details

    • slicingText

      private static ArrayList<String> slicingText(String originalString)
      Slices the original text into processable chunks for the dialog system. Normal text is separated from formatting tags enclosed in $.
      Parameters:
      originalString - the raw text containing plain text and formatting tags
      Returns:
      a list of sliced text chunks

    • playDialogFromString

      public static void playDialogFromString(javafx.scene.text.TextFlow textFlow, javafx.scene.image.ImageView tutorialImage, javafx.scene.image.ImageView soiFaceImage, String dialogText, double charPerSecond)
      Plays a dialog sequence from a string into a TextFlow. The dialog text is rendered character-by-character over time. Special tags in the text are parsed to trigger sounds, pauses, or images dynamically.
      Parameters:
      textFlow - the TextFlow to display the text
      tutorialImage - the ImageView to display any associated tutorial images
      soiFaceImage - the ImageView to display Soi's face expression during dialog
      dialogText - the text containing the dialog, optionally with formatting tags
      charPerSecond - the speed at which characters appear

    • skip

      public static void skip()
      Skips the current dialog sequence.

    • isPlaying

      public static boolean isPlaying()
      Checks if a dialog is currently playing.
      Returns:
      true if playing, false otherwise
